summary: Tight one‑set with fiery Big River and strong jams through The Other One
blurb: The Arrowhead show packs a crisp, biting guitar on Big River and a high‑energy sequence from Terrapin Station into The Other One and Wharf Rat. Keith’s keyboards cut through clearly while the drum pair stays low‑key; Good Lovin’ is a weak spot.
review: On July 1, 1978 at Arrowhead Stadium the Dead delivered an unusually long single set that felt more like two halves stitched together. The band surged through a scorching Big River – Garcia’s guitar described as “crisp” and “wringing every ounce out of his axe” – before launching into a seamless Terrapin Station → Playing In The Band → Drums → Space → Estimated Prophet → The Other One → Wharf Rat marathon that kept the heat‑induced tuning woes at bay. Keith Godchaux’s keyboards sparkle on the highlighted numbers, and while Mickey Hart mostly stays out, the brief Drums>Space segment adds a low‑key texture appropriate for a festival setting. A few rough edges appear – Good Lovin’ falters and the extreme summer conditions nudged the band’s tuning – but overall the performance remains tight, energetic, and memorable, especially for fans of the mid‑summer jam flow.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: Both recordings note strong standout moments (Big River, The Other One, Wharf Rat) and praise Garcia’s guitar and Keith’s keyboards, but also point out a weak Good Lovin’ performance and heat‑related tuning issues, yielding mixed overall sentiment with a modest positive tilt.

best_recording.reason: Audience tape captures the unique single‑set flow with crystal‑clear ambience; despite occasional thinness it delivers the most complete representation of the performance.

key_highlights: Big River showcases Garcia’s crisp, fiery guitar work.
A high‑energy jam chain from Terrapin Station through The Other One and Wharf Rat keeps momentum despite summer heat.
Unique set configuration with Jack Straw placed mid‑first set and Terrapin/Drums opening the second half adds character.

band_performance.Jerry: Crisp, fiery solos on Big River, Estimated Prophet and Wharf Rat; described as wringing every ounce from his guitar.
band_performance.Brent: Keith’s keyboards cut through clearly on the highlighted numbers, adding sparkle to Big River and supporting the jam sections.
band_performance.Mickey: Mostly stays out of the mix; when present Drums>Space is low‑key due to festival constraints.
